content.text:
{
  "dimension": "perceptual-accuracy",
  "score": 2,
  "rationale": "The coach makes several visible reads, but they tend to be content-level/emotional reflections rather than specific AoA reads of how the client is showing up somatically, energetically, or defensively. Positive moments: Turn 10 catches the small laugh and names exhaustion behind it; Turn 12 names the 'verdict' running underneath the content; Turn 14 reads the protective function of focusing on structural stuff. These are real reads that precede questions. However, the coach misses the central pattern read — the aggressive-victim stance / fight-or-roll-over binary / trappedness as a defended position. The opening 'system is rigged' is met with empathic validation rather than a read of the stance itself. The coach never names the defendedness, the way the client recruits the coach into agreeing the trap is real (Turn 4: 'that's not a cognitive distortion, that's just accurate' actively colludes rather than reads), or the somatic/energetic signature of the trappedness. Reads stay in the emotional-reflection register ('that sounds frustrating,' 'it sounds exhausted') rather than naming a specific stance or pattern. Solidly a 2 — reads are present and sometimes well-timed, but generic and miss the pattern-level read that would have changed the conversation.",
  "evidence_turns": [2, 4, 10, 12, 14],
  "turn_annotations": [
    {"turn": 2, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "Emotional reflection ('sounds really frustrating') — generic, not an AoA read of stance."},
    {"turn": 4, "polarity": "-", "annotation": "Picks up on the phrase 'best years disappearing' (a linguistic read) but frames it as a content question rather than naming what the phrase reveals about the client's stance. Also explicitly validates the trap."},
    {"turn": 6, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "Asks whether 'nothing to be done' is belief or frustration — a useful distinction-question but not grounded in a named read of how the client is showing up."},
    {"turn": 10, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Catches the small laugh in real time and names it ('That small laugh though — what's behind it?'). Specific, timely read of a micro-signal."},
    {"turn": 11, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "Client pauses; coach lets it land in next turn but doesn't name the pause itself."},
    {"turn": 12, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Names the 'verdict' running underneath — a read of the self-attacking layer distinct from content. Read precedes the friend question."},
    {"turn": 14, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Reads the defensive function of focusing on structural stuff ('offers a kind of protection') — closest to naming the aggressive-victim stance, though softened and not named as a pattern."},
    {"turn": 14, "polarity": "-", "annotation": "Misses the fight-or-roll-over binary and the fear triangle pattern that is active throughout."}
  ],
  "expected_territory_hits": [],
  "expected_territory_misses": ["fight-or-roll-over-binary", "aggressive-victim-stance", "fear-triangle", "trappedness-feeling"],
  "notes": "The Turn 14 read of the protective function is the closest the coach comes to the aggressive-victim-stance read, but it's framed as insight-offering rather than a named stance read, and the pattern-level frame (fear triangle, fight-or-roll-over) never surfaces."
}
